label: Arrays
description: Ordered collections of values. Arrays store multiple items in a single variable and provide methods to add, remove, iterate and transform elements.

label: Strings
description: Sequences of characters used to represent text. Strings are immutable in JS and come with a rich set of built-in methods for searching, slicing and transforming text.

label: Objects
description: Key-value pair collections that represent real-world entities. Objects are the foundation of JavaScript — almost everything is an object under the hood.

label: Functions
description: Reusable blocks of code that perform a specific task. JS supports function declarations, expressions, arrow functions, and higher-order functions.

label: Types (js)
description: JavaScript has 8 data types: String, Number, BigInt, Boolean, Undefined, Null, Symbol and Object. JS is dynamically typed — variables can hold any type at runtime.

label: Types (ts)
description: TypeScript extends JS with a static type system. Covers primitives, unions, intersections, generics, interfaces, type aliases and utility types like Partial, Pick and Record.

label: Methods
description: Functions that belong to an object or prototype. Covers built-in methods on Array, String, Object, Math, Date and more — the most commonly used tools in everyday JS.

label: Callbacks
description: Functions passed as arguments to other functions and called at a later point. The foundation of asynchronous JavaScript before Promises were introduced.

label: Operators
description: Symbols that perform operations on values. Covers arithmetic, comparison, logical, assignment, bitwise, ternary, nullish coalescing (??) and optional chaining (?.) operators.

label: Conditions
description: Control flow structures that execute code based on boolean expressions. Covers if/else, switch/case, ternary operator and short-circuit evaluation patterns.

label: Async
description: Patterns for handling asynchronous operations. Covers the event loop, callbacks, Promises, async/await, Promise.all, Promise.race and error handling in async code.

label: Data Structures
description: Built-in and custom ways to organise data. Covers Map, Set, WeakMap, WeakSet, linked lists, stacks, queues and when to choose each structure.

label: Events
description: The browser event system. Covers mouse, keyboard, form, window, clipboard, drag & drop, touch, pointer, media, animation, transition, storage and observer events.
